steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: The GCF of $16x^3y^2$ and $14xy$ is $2xy$. Factor it out:$$16x^3y^2+14xy=2xy(8x^2y+7)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $2xy(8x^2y+7)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Since $2xy$ is a common factor of each term in the given expression, the expression can be rewritten as $2xy\left(8{x}^{2}y+7\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $16{x}^{2}{y}^{2}+14xy$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $28{x}^{3}{y}^{2}+14xy$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $112{x}^{3}{y}^{2}+14xy$.
